Greet the customer with genuine energy - your first 5 seconds set the tone

Don't interrupt - even if you know the answer - let them finish
Say 'absolutely' and 'of course' - they signal willingness and partnership
Avoid monotone - vary pitch like you're telling a story to a friend

Be conversational - 'Yeah absolutely' sounds human - not robotic
Use inclusive 'we' language instead of 'you' - feels like teamwork

Start your greeting with positive words - reps scoring 100 on positive words open with 'Thank you for being a part of APS - my name is [your name] - how may I help you today?' then once the customer shares why they called respond with 'Absolutely I can definitely help with that'
Replace 'unfortunately' with 'what I can do is...' to focus on solutions
Never say 'I don't know' - say 'Great question let me find that answer for you'
Avoid 'but' - use 'and' or 'however' to sound less contradictory

Keep a personal attendance journal - notice your own patterns
Listen for the REAL issue behind the question - sometimes billing question is actually payment plan need
End every call with specific next steps - don't leave customer wondering what happens next
Follow up on promises - if you said you'd call back - call back

Be proactive - if you see a potential issue on account - address it before they ask
